title: Guillain-Barré syndrome - NHS
text:
infection, your immune system normally attacks and kills the bacteria or virus. But in Guillain-Barré syndrome the immune system starts to attack your nerves.
Guillain-Barré syndrome can affect anyone, but it's more common in adults and males.
Support if you have Guillain-Barré syndrome 
Get help from NHS talking therapies Having Guillain-Barré syndrome can affect your mental health. It might help to talk about how you're feeling.
You can access talking therapies for free on the NHS. These services can help you find ways to cope.
Help is available in person, by video, over the phone or as an online course.
Find NHS talking therapies for anxiety and depression 
Support from charities You can also get support from charities such as Inflammatory Neuropathies UK .
